blocks.text: Gerald Ross, Chief Financial Officer, Woods LLC

This letter confirms that {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} has engaged Fernhollow Partners LLC to perform a working capital assessment. We appreciate the opportunity. The terms below govern the engagement. If any item does not match your understanding, please tell me before you sign.

Scope. We will assess the company's net working capital position and its behavior over the trailing twelve months. The work covers accounts receivable, accounts payable, inventory, and accrued liabilities, together with the monthly close procedures that produce those balances. Our deliverable is a written report quantifying a normalized working capital level and identifying the drivers of variance around it. We will present the findings to you and, at your direction, to your board.

Approach. We work from your trial balance and sub-ledgers, reconciled monthly, and we tie every figure in the report to a source you can audit. Where the data does not support a conclusion, the report will say so. We expect roughly four months from start to final report, subject to timely access to records and staff.

Team. Sandra Perez, Director, leads the engagement day to day and is your first call on any question. Briana Dennis, Senior Associate, owns the analysis and the drafting. Ryan Strong, Analyst, supports the reconciliation and tie-out work. I review the model and the report before either reaches you.

Fee and timing. Our fee is a fixed {{fact:f:E-2023-001.fee}}, invoiced in equal monthly installments and payable on receipt. The fee covers the scope above; work beyond that scope requires a written amendment before it begins. Fieldwork begins {{fact:f:E-2023-001.start}}.
If these terms are acceptable, please countersign below and return one copy to my attention. We look forward to the work.
